description:
Offered: Fall
This course looks into the distinct rhetorical situation (audience, purpose, voice, etc.) of writing for organizations such as corporations and non-profits, both large and small, publicly traded and closely held. Coursework will extend beyond producing documents such as annual reports and codes of conduct, to also develop and practice literacies and skills that are flexible enough to be applied to a variety of situations and prepare students not only to succeed, but also to innovate. Cross-listed with ENGL 3860
Prerequisite(s): ENGL 1200
Note: Credit is not given for both COMM 3860 and ENGL 3860 .
Meets General Education Requirement(s): AIA, CV, IHIP
